Grund-G the veteran blandly ignores this 
kind of pressure, but the PCs ought to be easily, intimidated, Below is a list of the strategies Samson-G uses in distributing the semi-optional items. ‘As each item is distributed, the PCs may wish, to test the item or ask questions about is use. The designers and technicians will make a great show of being eager to cooperate, but if anything doesn’t work properly, they will bbe quick to blame it on the carelessness of the PCs or to make clever excuses about how sophisticated and delicate experimental equip ment is, At the same time the technicians will make certain that the PCs sign for each item they accept. "To make them propertly nervous, make each player actually sign an Information/Equip- ‘ment/Weapon Receipt Form scrap acknow!: edging acceptance of responsibility forthe ex ‘perimental tem received. Then, each time you temphasize the penalties for loss or damage ‘of experimental equipment, wave these pieces Cf paper in the air meaningfully. 
WW Strategies for Distributing Items 
Tactic 1: Samson-G reads a serial number 
aloud and asks for a volunteer. (Test of faith, loyalty, and stupidity.) 
Tactic 2: Samson-G speaks to each PC in turn and asks what they think they will need for this mission, (Be aler for any implied criticism fof The Computer's planning) Samson-G grils them — gives them a hard time about precious resources and the dedication and labor of 
RAD. After each PC has stated his desires, Samson asks the PC to pick a number from 
1-12, The PC is then assigned that item, 
Tactic 3: Any cally entertaining item not selected this way should be assigned at ran: 
dom. Don’t omit the rocket boots. 
Note: When each item is handed out, the designer steps forward and explains how ‘wonderful the item is. I the PC tess the item ‘on the spot and it doesn’t work, the designer ‘will of course blame the PC for damaging the item. PCs may wish they had accepted the items in silence and tested them later out of, sight of the designer. 
Hi High Tech Toys to Select From 
The name of the designer is in parentheses next tothe item, The PCs may lear that some 
designers are more reliable than others, and Use ths information to steer clear of dangerous, ‘useless items. 
1, Personal Anti-Grav Unit: Seri PDQ.R&D-124-56 (Stanley-B): This simply doesn’t work, and never will. if the PCs try to test it, the technicians (afraid to offend the research head) will continue fooling with it, swearing that it was ust working a minute ago. If the unit is dismantled and inspected by an independent, highly-skilled technician, it would be excellent evidence that Stanley-B is a phony, and therefore a traitor. 
2, Remote Control Surveillance Drone: PDQ- R&D-15-934 Schnect8): Ths sa mobile, n- telligent vid and sound. camera. which transmis to any standard multcorder. t moves at walk speed on lighterthar-air support and is driven by two small propeller motors; the drone can be operated witha contol joystick or by simple verbal commands. The drone is limited to line-ot sight for joystick control and shouting distance for verbal contol. tis semi- mar, about like a puppy, and equally in need Of training. It works fine when lab techs demonstrate it, but gett inthe field, and look ‘out — sort of a cheerful miniciigible with Cuisinart attachments 3. WaldoiMechano Exoskeleton: PDQ-R&D- 345 (Schnect8): This permits manipulation of heavy loads and high-speed movement — equivalent to. 30 Strength and. 2x normal ‘movement. Malfunctions al GM discretion — ideally, ater PC has been suckered into beliew- ing it eally works, (Suggestions: accidentally Crushes. what it manipulates; throttle gets stuck, ater sress limbs get locked in position; develops a nervous twitch.) PCs will get a few hints of bugs as itis demonstrated, but in general it seems to work fine, Heh, heh, 
4, Power Armor: PDQ-R&D-34-98 (designer tunknown: salvaged from abandoned Old Reckoning armed forces base): This is abs Solutely effective; the only problem is that it ‘weighs one ton and occasionally falls through the flooring of the complex. It is also credibly slow and poorly maneuverable. Iti ait balky in first gear, a8 PCs will note in test runs; in the field, twill sometimes refuse to move forward uniess coaxed, (Roll 1-8 to statin fist gear, Other problems: the armor can slow only by one gear per turn; itis tunstable in third gear.) Performan Ist gear Stroll (oll 1-8 to start) 2nd gear Walk 3rd gear Run (1-10 for steering ron) The armor provides ALL protection. The power armor has a builtin blaster that fires along arm; the blaster fring button is con- Cealed inside a finger of one glove. As you might well expect, the firing button gets stuck in the firing position the third time its used. Itcannot be turned off without the user remov- ing the suit and somebody making a x'% me. cchanical engineering skill rol. (It takes 1 to 20 rounds (rll to determine how many) to get into or out of the suit, during which time the blaster will continue to fire merrily.) ‘The suit’s power pack is defective, and will tun down when the GM wants some laughs as the PCs figure out how they are going to carry this one-ton valuable piece of ex- perimental equipment back to Alpha Complex. 
5, Poison Spray: PDQ-R&D-75-90 (Stanley-B) This is essentially a spray can full of poison, The PCs will not be permitted to test it indoors; the techs assure the PCs that it works fine. The ccan actually is under too high a pressure and has too tight a nozzle. it makes an incredibly fine spray which covers everything; its very

9, Rocket Boots: PDQ-R&D-82-81 (Stanley- B): This is the mad scientists favorite project; the PCs will be relentlessly browbeaten into testing these deathtraps. The boots have twelve individual rackets on each foot, each independently controlled by switches on the control belt. f used anywhere with a ceiling, lower than 20 feet, the user wll crash into the ceiling before gaining control.
